concerns.label: Sequential ablation conflates ordering effects with individual contributions
evidence: Table 1: proprioception-to-VLM result (87.7% LIBERO-plus) evaluated after multi-view already adopted (80.5%)
Table 1: soft-vs-tight VLM-policy connection (+0.8%) evaluated after Qwen3-VL-2B swap (+7.5%)
No cross-ablation or factorial design provided
concerns.decision_relevance: Limits causal interpretation of individual design choices; 'which choices matter' claim is overstated

concerns.label: Backbone confound in Table 2 comparison
evidence: VLANeXt uses Qwen3-VL-2B; OpenVLA-OFT uses LLaMA backbone
Table 1 shows LLaMA→Qwen3VL-2B adds 8% LIBERO / 8.7% LIBERO-plus on its own
VLANeXt vs OpenVLA-OFT margin: only 0.3% on LIBERO avg, ~10% on LIBERO-plus
Missing control: OpenVLA-OFT architecture + Qwen3-VL-2B backbone
concerns.decision_relevance: Cannot attribute LIBERO-plus improvement to architectural recipe vs backbone strength

concerns.label: Temporal history finding conflicts with other VLA work; may be LIBERO-specific
evidence: Table 1: temporal history hurts on LIBERO (91.8→85.0%) and LIBERO-plus (56.2→50.2%)
LIBERO tasks are predominantly single-step manipulation where temporal context adds noise

strengths: Clear, actionable recipe with large-effect findings (multi-view: +23pp on LIBERO-plus)
Real-world validation on 4 tasks across two robot platforms
Planned codebase release as community resource
Frequency-domain auxiliary loss: large gain (+4.1pp) with negligible compute
reasoning_steps: 1. Read full PDF: abstract, §2 (design space), Table 1 (sequential ablation), Table 2 (comparison), Table 3 (LIBERO-plus), §4 (real-world)
2. Identified sequential ablation as the key methodology concern — each finding is compound
3. Cross-referenced Table 1 backbone row vs Table 2 comparison to quantify backbone confound
4. Noted temporal history finding as the most counterintuitive, most in need of isolated replication
5. Confirmed that the comment adds something absent from the single existing comment (which was generic novelty dismissal)
